description: Whether the provider supports OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote MCP servers, including token storage and automatic refresh.

amp.mechanism: oauth_support: Amp supports OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote MCP servers

claude-code.mechanism: mcp_oauth_authentication: OAuth 2.0 for HTTP MCP servers; dynamic client registration or CIMD auto-discovery; token storage in macOS keychain or credentials file; auto-refresh; oauth.scopes pins requested scope set; authServerMetadataUrl overrides discovery chain

cline.mechanism: Cline MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote servers

codex.mechanism: mcp_oauth_support: Codex supports OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote MCP servers

copilot-cli.mechanism: Copilot CLI MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication

crush.mechanism: Crush MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote servers

cursor.mechanism: Remote servers using the streamable-http transport support OAuth authentication; static client credentials can be used instead of dynamic registration. The documentation explicitly notes OAuth as a streamable-http capability.

factory-droid.mechanism: OAuth authentication supported for HTTP servers; tokens stored globally in system keyring rather than per-project; managed via /mcp interactive manager

gemini-cli.mechanism: oauth_authentication: Gemini CLI supports OAuth 2.0 for remote MCP servers via SSE or HTTP transports; authProviderType selects dynamic_discovery, google_credentials, or service_account_impersonation; tokens stored in ~/.gemini/mcp-oauth-tokens.json and auto-refreshed

kiro.mechanism: Kiro MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication; remote server auth uses static headers

opencode.mechanism: Remote MCP servers support OAuth 2.0 authentication with Dynamic Client Registration (RFC 7591). OpenCode auto-detects 401 responses and initiates the OAuth flow, storing tokens in ~/.local/share/opencode/mcp-auth.json. Pre-registered client credentials can be supplied via the oauth object (clientId, clientSecret, scope). OAuth can be disabled per-server by setting oauth: false.

roo-code.mechanism: No OAuth 2.0 authentication flow documented for Roo Code MCP servers

windsurf.mechanism: Windsurf supports OAuth for each transport type (stdio, Streamable HTTP, and SSE) for remote MCP server authentication

zed.mechanism: When a remote MCP server is configured with a url but no Authorization header, Zed prompts the user to authenticate using the standard MCP OAuth flow
